name: Optimal: Dummy Node Iteration
summary: Place a dummy sentinel before the head so head-deletion needs no special case, then walk once with a single pointer, bypassing every node whose value matches — O(n) time, O(1) extra space.
visualizationIntent: init: values built as dummy(0) + input nodes with sequential forward arrows; links = next-index array; current pointer sits on the dummy (index 0); removed all-zero. loop: test whether current has a next node (links[current] != -1). check: read nxt = current.next and compare values[nxt] to val — nxt node highlighted as the candidate; on match it turns 'error' (red), else it will be kept. update-remove (match): current's arrow reroutes past the matched node to links[nxt] (changed_links fires — the bypass is the key visual), the matched node is marked removed (red, detached). move (no match): current label glides forward onto nxt. return: walk the surviving chain from the dummy and show result as the cleaned value list.

lineExplanations.3: Prepend a dummy sentinel node (value 0) in front of the real values. This sentinel is why the head itself can be deleted with the exact same logic as any other node.
lineExplanations.4: Build the next-pointer array: node i initially points to node i+1, and the last node points to -1 (which means null / end of list).
lineExplanations.5: A per-node flag array — a 1 marks a node that has been deleted. This drives the red 'removed' styling; it has no effect on the algorithm.
lineExplanations.6: Will hold the single arrow that was just rerouted this step, so the renderer can flash it.
lineExplanations.7: Start the walking pointer at the dummy (index 0). We always examine current's NEXT node, never current itself.
lineExplanations.8: Keep looping while current still has a next node — i.e. its next-pointer is not -1 (null).
lineExplanations.9: Look at the candidate: nxt is the node directly after current.
lineExplanations.10: Does the candidate node hold the value we're removing?
lineExplanations.11: Delete it: reroute current's next-pointer to skip the candidate and point straight at whatever came after it.
lineExplanations.12: Detach the removed node's own next-pointer (set to -1) so it dangles off the live list.
lineExplanations.13: Mark the candidate as removed — the renderer turns it red.
lineExplanations.14: Record that current's arrow is the one that just changed, so it flashes this step.
lineExplanations.16: The candidate survives, so advance current onto it and inspect the following node next time.
lineExplanations.17: No arrow changed this step, so clear the flash marker.
lineExplanations.18: Prepare an empty list to collect the values that survived.
lineExplanations.19: Start reading after the dummy — links[0] is the new head of the cleaned list.
lineExplanations.20: Walk until we run off the end of the surviving chain (-1).
lineExplanations.22: Follow the next-pointer to the next surviving node.
lineExplanations.23: Return the cleaned list of values — the new head onward.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: declares the class wrapper the platform runs.
syntaxExplanations.2: def removeElements(self, head, val): defines the method; head is the input list of node values, val is the value to remove.
syntaxExplanations.3: [0] + head is list concatenation — a brand-new list that starts with 0 and then contains every element of head.
syntaxExplanations.4: range(1, len(values)) yields 1,2,…,n-1; list(...) collects them into a list; + [-1] appends a final -1.
syntaxExplanations.5: [0] * len(values) repeats the list [0] len(values) times, producing a same-length list of zeros.
syntaxExplanations.7: = assigns the integer 0 to the variable current.
syntaxExplanations.8: while … : repeats the indented block while the condition holds; != means 'not equal to'.
syntaxExplanations.9: links[current] reads the element of the list links at index current.
syntaxExplanations.10: if … : runs its block only when the condition is true; == tests equality.
syntaxExplanations.11: Copies one list element (links[nxt]) into another position (links[current]).
syntaxExplanations.12: Stores -1 into links at index nxt.
syntaxExplanations.13: Stores 1 into removed at index nxt.
syntaxExplanations.14: [current] is a one-element list holding the value of current.
syntaxExplanations.15: else: introduces the branch taken when the if condition above is false.
syntaxExplanations.16: = reassigns current to the value of nxt.
syntaxExplanations.17: Resets changed_links back to an empty list.
syntaxExplanations.18: Creates a new empty list named result.
syntaxExplanations.19: links[0] reads the first element of links.
syntaxExplanations.20: while … : loops while walk is not equal to -1.
syntaxExplanations.21: .append(...) adds one item onto the end of the list result.
syntaxExplanations.22: Follows the link by reading links at index walk.
syntaxExplanations.23: return hands result back as the function's output.
